stem:
f(t) = 36t − 3t²

The function f is defined by the given equation. The function g is defined by g(t) = f(t) + 5. Which expression represents the maximum value of g(t)?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The function f(t) = −3t² + 36t is a downward-opening parabola with vertex at t = 36/(2·3) = 6. The maximum value is f(6) = 36(6) − 3(6)² = 216 − 108 = 108, which can be written as 3(36/6)². Since g(t) = f(t) + 5, the maximum of g is 5 + 3(36/6)².

Choices A and D use a wrong vertex location 36/3. Choice C subtracts instead of adds the positive maximum of f.
